name: HireQuest, Inc.
business: HireQuest is a nationwide franchisor of temporary staffing offices providing direct-dispatch and commercial staffing solutions in the light industrial and blue-collar industries, and professional recruitment offices providing permanent placement services. Revenue primarily consists of royalty fees from franchisees.

exposures.sentence: We are a nationwide franchisor of temporary staffing offices providing direct-dispatch and commercial staffing solutions in the light industrial and blue-collar industries, and professional recruitment offices providing permanent placement services in the executive, managerial and administrative fields.
They are in the process of integrating AI technology into all of our systems which, we believe, will allow our franchisees to better reach potential customers and temporary employees.
The improper disclosure of or access to our confidential and proprietary information, or a failure to adequately protect this information, could materially harm our business.

Our clients increasingly rely on automation, artificial intelligence, machine learning, and other new and rapidly evolving technologies to reduce their dependence on labor needs, which may reduce demand for our services.

catalysts.sentence: We will perform our next annual goodwill impairment tests as of August 31, 2026; or earlier, if adverse changes in circumstances result in our assessment that a triggering event has occurred at any of our reporting units and an interim test is required.
The Senior Credit Facility will mature on February 28, 2028.

tamClaims.claim: The U.S. staffing and recruiting industry generated between $190 billion and $225 billion in revenue in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the American Staffing Association ("ASA"), the staffing and recruiting industry in the United States generated record annual revenue of between approximately $190 to $225 billion in 2025.

tamClaims.claim: 75% to 90% of industry revenue is from temporary and contract staffing.
tamClaims.sentence: Approximately 75% to 90% of industry revenue is generated by temporary and contract employee staffing services, with the remainder coming from executive recruiting and permanent placement, outsourcing / outplacement, and human resource consulting.
